title: IBPS PO 2026
meta_description: IBPS PO 2026 — Probationary Officer exam for public sector banks. Prelims/Mains pattern, salary ₹36-63K/month, eligibility, preparation tips, and syllabus.

hero.one_liner: Probationary Officer recruitment for 11 public sector banks — one of India's most popular banking exams with excellent salary and career growth

what_is_it: IBPS PO (Institute of Banking Personnel Selection — Probationary Officer) is an annual recruitment exam for the post of Probationary Officer / Management Trainee in 11 participating public sector banks including Bank of Baroda, Canara Bank, Indian Bank, Punjab National Bank, Union Bank of India, Bank of India, Central Bank of India, Indian Overseas Bank, UCO Bank, Bank of Maharashtra, and Punjab & Sind Bank (SBI conducts its own separate PO exam).

The exam has three stages: Prelims (online, screening), Mains (online, merit), and Interview. Total process takes about 4-5 months from notification to final allotment. As a Probationary Officer, you join at Junior Management Grade Scale-I (JMGS-I) with a starting basic pay of ₹36,000/month plus DA, HRA, and other allowances — total in-hand salary is ₹42,000-63,000/month depending on posting location.

IBPS PO is one of the most competitive banking exams with 15-25 lakh applicants for about 4,000-5,000 vacancies. However, unlike UPSC, the success rate is higher if you're well-prepared — the exam tests speed and accuracy rather than deep knowledge. A focused 4-6 month preparation with regular mocks is sufficient for most candidates.

Career growth in banking is excellent: PO → Senior Manager → Chief Manager → AGM → DGM → GM → ED → MD&CEO. Promotions are time-bound initially and merit-based later. Many bank officers also appear for promotional exams to accelerate growth. Bank POs enjoy job security, pension (under NPS), medical benefits, subsidized housing loans, and postings across India.

key_details.label: Education
key_details.value: Bachelor's degree from any recognized university (any stream). Final year students can apply.

key_details.label: Age Limit
key_details.value: 20–30 years (General). OBC: up to 33, SC/ST: up to 35, PwBD: up to 40.

key_details.label: Selection Process
key_details.value: Prelims → Mains → Interview → Final Merit (80% Mains + 20% Interview)

key_details.label: Participating Banks
key_details.value: 11 public sector banks (NOT SBI — SBI has separate exam)

key_details.label: Posting
key_details.value: Initially at branch level. Can be posted anywhere in India under the allocated bank.

exam_pattern.description: Online exam with 100 MCQs in 60 minutes. Three sections with individual sectional cutoff AND overall cutoff. This is purely qualifying — Prelims marks don't count in final merit.

Online objective exam (200 marks, 3 hours) + Descriptive paper (25 marks, 30 minutes). The objective paper has 4 sections with individual time limits. Descriptive paper tests English essay and letter writing.

subjects.name: Descriptive: Essay + Letter (30 min, pen-on-paper or typing)

exam_pattern.negative_marking: 0.25 marks per wrong answer (objective). Descriptive is evaluated only if you clear objective cutoff.

extra_sections.heading: Salary Breakdown — What You Actually Get
content: Basic Pay: ₹36,000/month (starting), increasing to ₹63,840 with annual increments.
Dearness Allowance (DA): ~50% of basic (revised quarterly based on inflation) = ₹18,000+
House Rent Allowance (HRA): Metro: 15% of basic. Other cities: 7.5-10%. If bank accommodation provided, HRA not given.
City Compensatory Allowance (CCA): ₹870-1,460/month depending on city classification.
Other allowances: Special allowance (~16% of basic), transport allowance, telephone reimbursement, newspaper allowance, medical aid.
Total in-hand (approximate): Metro posting (Mumbai/Delhi): ₹55,000-63,000/month. Tier-2 city: ₹45,000-55,000/month. Semi-urban: ₹42,000-48,000/month.

Additional benefits: Subsidized home loan (2% below market rate), vehicle loan at concessional rate, medical insurance for family, pension (NPS), gratuity, leave fare concession, children's education allowance.

extra_sections.heading: 6-Month Preparation Strategy for IBPS PO
content: Month 1-2 (Foundation): Build basics in Quantitative Aptitude (percentage, ratio, profit-loss, SI/CI, time-work-distance), Reasoning (puzzles, seating arrangement, blood relations, coding, syllogisms), and English (reading comprehension, grammar, vocabulary). Use RS Aggarwal for Quant and Verbal/Non-Verbal Reasoning.

Month 3-4 (Practice + Banking Awareness): Start intensive practice — solve 50+ questions daily across all sections. Begin banking awareness preparation: read 'Banking Awareness' by Arihant, learn about RBI policies, banking terminologies, recent mergers, financial inclusion schemes, digital banking. Read The Hindu Business Line for economy/banking news.

Month 5 (Mocks + Descriptive): Take 3-4 full-length mock tests per week. Focus on time management — you have strict sectional time limits. Practice Descriptive paper: write 1 essay (250 words) and 1 formal letter per day. Topics usually relate to banking, economy, social issues, or government schemes.

Month 6 (Revision + Interview Prep): Revise all formulas, shortcuts, and banking awareness notes. Take 5 mocks per week. For interview preparation: know your bio-data form inside out, prepare for 'Why banking?' question, understand the bank you're allocated to, and practice group discussion (some banks have GD before interview).

preparation_tips: Time management is everything in IBPS PO. Prelims gives you 60 minutes for 100 questions — that's 36 seconds per question. Mains has strict sectional time limits. Practice with a timer from day one.

Banking Awareness is the differentiator. Most candidates are strong in Quant and Reasoning (same syllabus as SSC). Banking Awareness is where you gain an edge — learn RBI functions, monetary policy, types of accounts, NPA, Basel norms, RTGS/NEFT/UPI, and recent banking news.

Don't ignore the Descriptive paper (25 marks). It's evaluated only if you clear the objective cutoff, but many candidates lose their selection due to poor descriptive scores. Practice writing a 250-word essay in 15 minutes and a formal letter in 10 minutes.

Puzzles and Seating Arrangement dominate the Reasoning section (15-20 questions out of 35-45). Master these question types — they're time-consuming but learnable with practice. Practice 5 puzzle sets daily.
Apply for IBPS PO even if you're preparing for SBI PO — the syllabus is 90% similar. The experience of one exam helps in the other. Many candidates clear both and then choose based on final offer.

faqs.question: What degree is needed for IBPS PO?
faqs.answer: Any Bachelor's degree from a UGC-recognized university. No minimum percentage required (though some banks may have their own internal thresholds). Final year students can apply — degree must be completed before the common interview date.

faqs.question: Is IBPS PO harder than SSC CGL?
faqs.answer: Different, not necessarily harder. IBPS PO tests speed and accuracy under extreme time pressure (36 sec/question in Prelims). SSC CGL tests depth of knowledge across a wider syllabus. Banking Awareness in IBPS PO is an additional subject not in SSC CGL. Many students prepare for both simultaneously since Quant and Reasoning overlap.

faqs.question: Can I choose which bank I get posted to?
faqs.answer: You fill a preference list of banks during application. Bank allocation depends on your rank, preferences, and available vacancies. Higher rank = more choice. Within the allocated bank, your posting location is decided by the bank — you can prefer a zone but specific branch posting is at the bank's discretion.

faqs.question: What is the career growth for a bank PO?
faqs.answer: PO (Scale I) → Senior Manager (Scale II, 3-4 years) → Chief Manager (Scale III, 5-6 years) → AGM (Scale IV) → DGM (Scale V) → GM (Scale VI) → ED (Scale VII) → MD & CEO. Time-bound promotions for the first 2-3 levels, merit-based thereafter. Many bank officers also move to RBI, NABARD, SEBI through lateral recruitment.

faqs.question: Is there an interview in IBPS PO?
faqs.answer: Yes. Unlike SSC exams, IBPS PO has an interview round carrying about 20% weightage in the final merit (100 marks interview out of 100). The interview tests your personality, banking knowledge, current affairs, and suitability for a banking career. Dress formally, know your DAF well, and be ready to discuss 'Why banking?'

faqs.question: SBI PO vs IBPS PO — which is better?
faqs.answer: SBI PO is considered slightly more prestigious and pays marginally higher due to SBI being India's largest bank. However, IBPS PO offers posting in 11 banks, giving more options for preferred location. SBI PO has a separate exam (conducted by SBI, not IBPS). Preparation is largely similar. Many aspirants appear for both.
